title: hedyotis pseudocorymbosa
intro: Hedyotis pseudocorymbosa, also known as the ' False St. John's Wort' or 'Rou kon', is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Hedyotis pseudocorymbosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antioxidant properties. Hedyotis pseudocorymbosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies like poultices and ointments.
